feedback.text: I think the Digital Fairness act is a great leap forward towards our customer rights. But It also fails in few specific aspects. Mainly, it fails to include recent initiative called "Stop Killing Games", which has similar goals to this act, but just goes a tiny step further. I think people should own things they purchase digitally and that service should either not end or have an alternative, so people can still access the stuff they paid for. Just as you can still drive a car after 5 years, you should still be able to use that program or play that videogame. Even if it means no further updates for you or disconnect from advanced server based features ( like the use of server-side AI or cloud ). When it comes to videogames, that means that things like self hosting, which the studios making those games already have, because of inner testing and all the developers confirm this, so should the customer have such an option. They also say that it is potential liability for them, which it isnt, they can just add to their EULA, that they are not responsible for damaged caused by third parties. So I think this should be slightly expanded to include this very important and popular initiative. Just to edit and add... I think there should also be European payment processor, one that will not use its dominant power to censor people, like Visa and Mastercard are doing right now, denying service to LEGAL content online, like in the case of Steam and Itch.io
